rulings.Case Title: MY NGUYEN VS. LINK DEPOT CORP. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Amended Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 4, 2024, Line 2. PLAINTIFF MY NGUYEN's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. Plaintiff's unopposed motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SANDOVAL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or In The Alternative Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 4, 2024, Line 7. PLAINTIFF RICHARD SANDOVAL's Motion To Strike Or In The Alternative Tax Costs. Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc. (AHM) won a lemon law jury trial, then sought its costs by memorandum. Plaintiff moved to strike or tax certain of the costs. (Not. 1:9-14.) Plaintiff's motion is adjudged as follows. Filing fees ($954). Motions need not be granted to be "reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation." (CCP 1033.5(c).) Plaintiff's motion is denied. Deposition charges ($1,200). AHM provides insufficient support for this amount of deposition charges. (Opp. 5:8-16.) Plaintiff's motion is granted. Service of process charges ($365). AHM provides insufficient support for this amount of these charges. (Opp. 5:17-24.) Plaintiff's motion is granted. Expert fees ($14,042). Plaintiff rejected AMH's $10,000 CCP 998 offer, thus opening himself to post-offer expert fees. That is not a token offer in a single-vehicle lemon law case. Moreover, the AHM expert's hourly rate ($381) and time worked (31 hours) were reasonable. Plaintiff's motion is denied. Other charges ($8,522). The court exercises its discretion to strike these charges not authorized by statute. Plaintiff's motion is granted. In sum, AHM's cost memorandum is stricken/taxed $10,087. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CONLIZA PANG VS. NEW CENTURY CUSTOM CABINETS, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 4, 2024, Line 8. DEFENDANT NEW CENTURY CUSTOM CABINETS' DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled. The demurrer says the FAC is "barred by the statute of limitationand the delayed discovery rule does not apply." (Dmr. Not. 2:6-7.) However, the FAC adequately pleads the delayed discovery rule, and it is a factual issue that can be explored in discovery. (Id. at 3:25-4:13; Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 807, 810.) To the extent the demurrer separately attacks the fraud count on specificity grounds, that count is adequately pled. (See, e.g., FAC 3:12-15.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
